prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white.

At the opening frame, the image is very dark and low-contrast. Only vague shapes are visible, suggesting an indoor setting, but the details are not clear enough to identify objects or people confidently. On the right side there appears to be a series of curved or vertical forms, but their identity is unclear.

By the final frame, the commercial has shifted to a bright graphic title card. Large bold text in the center reads “OIL HEAT.” Above the words is a simple line drawing resembling the pitched roof of a house. Beneath the main text, in much smaller lettering, it reads “NATIONAL OIL FUEL INSTITUTE.” The image has a clean, poster-like layout with black text and lines on a light background.
